items.text:
NOTICE: Elevator Maintenance — Harrington Residence Hall

Scheduled service will be performed on both elevators in Harrington Hall from Monday, October 6 through Wednesday, October 8. Work will take place each day between 8:00 AM and 5:00 PM. During these hours, both elevators will be taken out of operation and residents must use the north and south stairwells.

Please note the following:

- Elevator A is expected to reopen by Tuesday afternoon; Elevator B will remain closed until Wednesday evening.
- Residents who require accessibility assistance may request a temporary ground-floor room by emailing housing@campus.edu before October 3.
- Large deliveries should be rescheduled, as no freight access will be available during service.
- A $25 fee will be charged for any furniture left blocking the stairwells, which must stay clear for safety.

We apologize for the inconvenience. Questions may be directed to the Resident Manager, Mr. Alvarez, at Room 110 or by calling the front desk at extension 4400.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what will happen to Elevator B during the maintenance period?

options.A: It will reopen on Tuesday afternoon.
options.B: It will be replaced with a freight lift.
options.C: It will operate only after 5:00 PM.
options.D: It will stay closed until Wednesday evening.

questions.explanation: The bulleted list states that Elevator A is expected to reopen by Tuesday afternoon, while Elevator B will remain closed until Wednesday evening. Option A swaps the two elevators, and the others introduce conditions not stated.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a resident who uses a wheelchair?

options.A: They will be charged the $25 fee during the work.
options.B: They must move all deliveries to the stairwells.
options.C: They should contact the housing office before October 3.
options.D: They are expected to wait in the lobby each day.

questions.explanation: Since both elevators are out of service and stairwells must be used, a resident needing accessibility assistance would rely on the offer to request a ground-floor room by emailing housing@campus.edu before October 3. The other options misapply details from the notice.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To announce a permanent closure of one stairwell.
options.B: To inform residents about a temporary elevator service.
options.C: To collect a furniture fee from every resident.
options.D: To advertise new accessibility rooms on the ground floor.

questions.explanation: The notice overall informs residents that both elevators will be serviced over three days and explains the resulting arrangements. Options A, C, and D each seize on a single detail rather than the central purpose.

items.text:
NOTICE: Holiday Hours at the Campus Mailroom

The Student Mailroom in Bradley Center will operate on a reduced schedule during the Thanksgiving break, from Wednesday, November 26 through Sunday, November 30. Regular hours will resume on Monday, December 1.

During the break, the mailroom will be open as follows:

- Wednesday, November 26: 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM only
- Thursday and Friday, November 27-28: closed
- Saturday, November 29: 1:00 PM to 4:00 PM

Packages that arrive while the mailroom is closed will be held securely and may be picked up when normal service resumes. Students must present a valid ID card to collect any parcel. A $3 storage fee will be applied to packages left uncollected for more than ten days after notification.

Please check your campus portal for pickup notices, and plan ahead if you expect a delivery during this period. For urgent questions, contact the mailroom supervisor, Ms. Park, at the Bradley Center service window.

format_metadata.title: Holiday Hours at the Campus Mailroom

questions.stem: According to the notice, when is the mailroom open on Saturday, November 29?
questions.explanation: The schedule lists Saturday, November 29 as 1:00 PM to 4:00 PM. Option A uses the Wednesday hours, while B and D give times that appear nowhere in the notice.

questions.stem: What must a student do to collect a parcel during the holiday period?

options.A: Pay a $3 fee at every pickup.

questions.explanation: The notice states that students must present a valid ID card to collect any parcel. The $3 fee applies only after ten days, contacting the supervisor is for urgent questions, and no arrival deadline is required for pickup.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a package that arrives on Thursday, November 27?

options.A: It will be held until service resumes.
options.B: It will be returned to the sender immediately.
options.C: It will incur a storage fee the same day.
options.D: It will be delivered to the student's room.

questions.explanation: The mailroom is closed on Thursday, November 27, and the notice says packages arriving while it is closed are held securely for later pickup. The other options contradict the stated handling of held packages.
